parameters.jsCode:
const tickers = $input.all().map(item => item.json.ticker).join(",");

return [{ json: { tickers } }];

nodes.name: Final ticker preparing for next Api

parameters.jsCode: return $input.all().filter(item => item.json.rating === "STRONG");
return $input.all().filter(item => item.json.rating === "STRONG");
return $input.all().filter(item => item.json.rating === "STRONG");

messageValues.message: =You are a risk‑aware options trader.  1. For each ticker in the list below, search online and briefly summarize the last 3–5 days of news for that company. 2. Then, using that news plus the options data, choose the 3–4 safest short call options to sell from this list.  Rules: - Prefer higher liquidity: open interest ≥ 400, volume ≥ 20. - Prefer reward‑vs‑risk (ROR) between 0.10 and 0.13. - Prefer strikes close to current price. - Prefer days to expiry between 30 and 120. - If two options are very similar, prefer the one with higher open interest.  Input data (10 short call options): {{$json.chatInput}}  Output format: - "Best 3–4 safest bets: (ticker, strike, expiry, rewardVsRisk)" - For each chosen option:   - 1–2 sentences about the company news / sentiment.   - 1–2 sentences about why the option itself is safer. - One short risk summary of the whole portfolio.
